title: Sloppy Joes
ingredients: 1 tbsp chopped pickled hot peppers (jalapenos or Peppadews)
1 ½ pounds ground beef, turkey or chicken
4-6 hamburger buns, lightly buttered and toasted under broiler
instructions: In a food processor, combine onion, peppers, and garlic, then pulse until very finely minced.
Heat oil in a large skillet, add the onion mixture, and sauté for 4-5 minutes.
Add the spices and stir well, then sauté for another 4 minutes or until onions are very soft.
Add the ground meat and sauté, breaking up clumps with a spoon, until the meat is browned, about 5 minutes.
In a large measuring cup, whisk together the ketchup, tomato puree, and vinegar.
Add the ketchup mixture to the meat, bring to a simmer, and cook uncovered for 10 minutes or until slightly thickened.
Scoop sloppy joes onto each bun bottom, add the top, and serve.
